tasks.description: Make DynamicEffectRegistry reactive: add _version counter, _listeners set, subscribe/getSnapshot arrow methods, _notify() private method, and batch() for suppressing notifications during bulk registration. Call _notify() at end of register(), registerAsync(), unregister() only when state actually changed. Extend dynamic record type to include optional schema?: ParameterSchema. Add getSchema(name) method. File: src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts
tasks.executor_notes: No further code changes were needed in this pass. The rerun suite kept the registry batching, schema storage, and stable subscribe/getSnapshot behavior green, so the prior DynamicEffectRegistry implementation still holds under the new integration coverage.

tasks.description: Wire reactivity into useEffectRegistry: (1) Add useSyncExternalStore(registry.subscribe, registry.getSnapshot) after the useMemo so InnerProvider re-renders when async registration completes. (2) For the first-render timing fix: use useLayoutEffect instead of useEffect for replaceEffectRegistry(registry) — this runs synchronously after render but before children paint, closing the first-render gap while keeping render pure. (3) Change the Promise.all registration to sequential-by-priority using registry.batch(): drafts first (lowest), then DB effects (with console.warn if db.length > 0 about legacy deprecation), then resource effects (highest, passing effect.parameterSchema). batch() suppresses notifications until all registrations complete, then fires one _notify(). File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts
tasks.executor_notes: Moved the singleton swap onto the hook render path by calling `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` immediately after memoizing the registry. The reproduction script and the new integration test both observed `same:missing` on first render and `same:found` after async registration, which confirms the module singleton is installed before child render and that `useSyncExternalStore` still drives the rerender path.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. This rework changed `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts`, and the file now matches the executor note: render-time `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)`, `useSyncExternalStore`, and batched drafts→DB→resources registration with schema forwarding.

tasks.description: Create EffectErrorBoundary class component. Props: { effectName: string; fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode }. On error: getDerivedStateFromError sets hasError: true. componentDidCatch logs '[EffectErrorBoundary] "{effectName}" runtime error: {error.message}'. render() returns this.props.fallback when errored, this.props.children otherwise. Reset when effectName changes (compare in getDerivedStateFromError or use key= at call sites). Keep under 50 lines. File: src/tools/video-editor/effects/EffectErrorBoundary.tsx

tasks.description: Create validateAndCoerceParams utility. Takes (params: Record<string,unknown> | undefined, schema: ParameterSchema | undefined) => Record<string,unknown>. Fast path: return params ?? {} if schema is undefined/empty. Otherwise: start with defaults built inline (replicating getFallbackValue logic from ParameterControls — do NOT import from the component to avoid layering inversion). Overlay user params with per-type coercion: number → typeof check + clamp(min,max), boolean → typeof check, select → validate against options, color → regex /^#[0-9a-f]{3,8}$/i. Wrong types fall back to default. File: src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts. Keep under 60 lines.
tasks.executor_notes: No new edits were required. The coercion assertions remained green in the updated DynamicEffectRegistry test module, so runtime param validation still behaves as implemented earlier.

watch_items: RENDER PURITY (correctness-1/5): Plan says replaceEffectRegistry() in render. Use useLayoutEffect instead — runs before paint but after render, keeping render pure. This is the safer alternative endorsed by the gate.

BATCH NOTIFICATIONS (performance-1, all_locations): DynamicEffectRegistry.batch(fn) must suppress _notify() calls during fn execution and fire one _notify() at the end. Without this, sequential registration causes O(n) InnerProvider re-renders.

PREVIEW FPS (FLAG-004, correctness-2, callers): EffectCreatorPanel has no fps prop and no context exposes it. Keep PREVIEW_FPS=30 hardcoded — this is an accepted tradeoff. Do NOT try to thread fps from context.

LAYERING INVERSION (conventions flag): validateParams.ts lives in effects/ — do NOT import getDefaultValues from components/ParameterControls.tsx. Duplicate the getFallbackValue logic inline in the utility to maintain layer separation.

EFFECT LAYER CONTRACT: EffectLayerSequence passes effectFrames={durationInFrames} for continuous effects. wrapWithClipEffects does NOT pass effectFrames for continuous (only for entrance/exit). Preserve both contracts exactly.
CONTINUOUS EFFECT in wrapWithClipEffects: Current code (line 129-136) does NOT pass effectFrames to continuous effects — only durationInFrames and intensity. Do not add effectFrames here.
BUILT-IN PRIORITY INVARIANT: DynamicEffectRegistry.get() returns builtIn[name] ?? dynamic[name]. This must remain — built-ins always win over dynamic effects with same name.
DEBT ITEMS — do not make worse: [DEBT] video-editor-effect-registry, [DEBT] video-editor-effect-preview, [DEBT] clip-param-schema-evolution
CustomEffectEditor.tsx is dead code (no callers) — do NOT modify or migrate it. Out of scope per SD-005.

meta_commentary:
Key execution decisions the plan leaves to the executor:

1. **useLayoutEffect over render-time mutation**: The plan says replaceEffectRegistry() in render (Step 1.2). The gate explicitly says useLayoutEffect is an acceptable safer alternative. USE useLayoutEffect — it runs synchronously before paint, closing the first-render gap without violating React purity. This satisfies the relaxed criterion: "module singleton available before first child render."

2. **batch() API design**: Add a simple `batch(fn: () => Promise<void>)` method to DynamicEffectRegistry that sets a `_batching = true` flag, awaits fn, then sets `_batching = false` and calls `_notify()` once. The `_notify()` method should early-return when `_batching` is true. This prevents O(n) re-renders during initialization.

3. **validateParams layering**: The plan says reuse getDefaultValues from ParameterControls. The conventions flag correctly identifies this as a layering inversion (effects/ importing from components/). Instead, duplicate the ~15 lines of getFallbackValue logic directly in validateParams.ts. This is deliberate duplication to maintain layer boundaries — it's the right call.

4. **Continuous effect contract divergence**: wrapWithClipEffects (index.tsx:128-136) does NOT pass effectFrames to continuous effects — only durationInFrames and intensity. EffectLayerSequence (line 42) passes effectFrames={durationInFrames}. Both are correct for their contexts. The preview's wrapPreviewEffect should use the EffectLayerSequence pattern for continuous (effectFrames=durationInFrames) since it's a standalone preview.

5. **Preview FPS**: Keep hardcoded at 30. Do NOT try to thread fps from context — accepted tradeoff, would require plumbing changes to ClipPanel and DataProviderContext that are out of scope.

6. **TypeScript**: registerAsync signature gains optional `schema?: ParameterSchema`. This is backward-compatible — all existing callers pass 2 args. Only resource registration passes the third arg.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Make DynamicEffectRegistry reactive with subscribe/getSnapshot + fix first-render timing
Step 2: Create EffectErrorBoundary with explicit fallback prop
Step 3: Wire EffectErrorBoundary into wrapWithClipEffects, EffectLayerSequence, EffectCreatorPanel
Step 4: Extend tests for reactivity, dedup, batch, error boundary, param validation
Step 5: Store parameterSchema in DynamicEffectRegistry alongside effects
Step 6: Create validateAndCoerceParams utility with type coercion
Step 7: Apply validateAndCoerceParams at render-time in wrapWithClipEffects and EffectLayerSequence
Step 8: Consolidate effect sources with explicit priority + deprecate useEffects.ts
Step 9: Align EffectCreatorPanel preview with timeline wrapping

validation.completeness_notes: All 11 plan steps mapped to tasks. Steps 5 and 1 are combined into T1 since both modify DynamicEffectRegistry.ts. Steps 7 and 3 share T5/T6 since error boundary wiring and param validation apply to the same call sites. Step 11 (manual smoke tests) is info-only per the plan and is noted in T10's scope.
